turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Hi doctor. I’m here for my blood pressure follow-up, and I’ve also got this dry cough that just won’t go away.
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, female adult, mild local accent, slightly anxious but clear speech, moderate pace. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Good morning. Let’s take a look at both. How long have you had the cough, and have you noticed anything else with it?
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, calm and professional, moderate pace, clear articulation. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: About two weeks now. It’s dry, mostly at night. No phlegm. It’s a bit annoying, but I’m otherwise okay.
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, female adult, mild local accent, slightly anxious but clear speech, moderate pace. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Any fever, shortness of breath, wheezing, chest pain, or coughing up blood?
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, calm and professional, moderate pace, clear articulation. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No, none of that. Just the cough. My throat feels a little tickly sometimes.
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, female adult, mild local accent, slightly anxious but clear speech, moderate pace. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: All right. Do you have a runny nose, sneezing, or post-nasal drip? And have your allergies been bothering you lately?
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, calm and professional, moderate pace, clear articulation. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Yes, actually. I’ve had some sneezing and a bit of a blocked nose. The pollen seems bad again.
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, female adult, mild local accent, slightly anxious but clear speech, moderate pace. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: That does sound like allergies. Are you taking the cetirizine regularly, or only when you need it?
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, calm and professional, moderate pace, clear articulation. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Only when I need it. I took it a few times this week, but not every day.
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, female adult, mild local accent, slightly anxious but clear speech, moderate pace. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Thank you. Let’s talk about your blood pressure now. Have you been taking the amlodipine every day? Any side effects like ankle swelling, dizziness, or flushing?
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, calm and professional, moderate pace, clear articulation. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: Yes, I take it daily. No real side effects. Maybe a little swelling in the evenings sometimes, but it’s mild.
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, female adult, mild local accent, slightly anxious but clear speech, moderate pace. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Okay. Do you know what your home readings have been, if you’ve checked them?
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, calm and professional, moderate pace, clear articulation. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: I checked a few times at the pharmacy. It was around 132 over 84 last week, and one reading was 128 over 80.
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, female adult, mild local accent, slightly anxious but clear speech, moderate pace. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Those are fairly good. I’d like to confirm with today’s reading here. Before I do that, any headaches, vision changes, palpitations, or swelling that’s getting worse?
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, calm and professional, moderate pace, clear articulation. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: patient
turns.text: No headaches or vision changes. The swelling is not bad, and the cough is really the main thing bothering me.
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, female adult, mild local accent, slightly anxious but clear speech, moderate pace. Style: neutral.

turns.speaker: doctor
turns.text: Understood. I’m thinking the cough is most likely from your allergies or post-nasal drip, rather than anything serious. Your blood pressure sounds reasonably controlled on the current dose. I’ll check your pressure now, and if it stays around this range, we can keep the amlodipine as is for the moment. For the cough, I’d suggest taking the cetirizine daily for the next couple of weeks, and using saline nasal rinses if you tolerate them. If the symptoms don’t improve, we can review whether you need a different allergy treatment. Please come back sooner if you get shortness of breath, chest pain, fever, coughing blood, or if the cough lasts beyond about 3 to 4 weeks. We’ll also keep an eye on the ankle swelling and your blood pressure at follow-up.
turns.style: Voice profile: South African English, calm and professional, moderate pace, clear articulation. Style: neutral.
